page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with hardware support for AVX-512 vector and vector neural network instructions (VNNI) alongside AES cryptography.

This server is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local NVMe storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached persistent disks. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and software compilation tasks placing in the top 25%. Extended CPU instruction tests and image processing also demonstrate strong performance. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations fall within the average range, while large-scale LLM text generation is weak, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration.

Given its high memory density and dedicated compute resources, the n2-highmem-64 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound enterprise applications that do not require local SSD performance or GPU acceleration. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, software compilation pipelines, and enterprise-grade web serving workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. With 32 physical cores and hyperthreading, it delivers strong multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation and image processing benchmarks. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding enterprise database and caching workloads.

Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz.
